input.background: An early publication from the Framingham Heart Study identified 51 incidences of coronary disease among 424 men with high serum cholesterol (245 mg per 100 mL and above) over a 6-year period. Among 454 men with lower cholesterol (less than 210 mg per 100 mL), there were 16 incident cases.
input.question: Calculate the incidence rates and the relative risk (RR) of coronary disease for men with high serum cholesterol compared to those with lower cholesterol.

output.method: 事件发生概率及独立性

high_cholesterol_cases.description: Number of coronary disease cases among men with high serum cholesterol (245 mg per 100 mL and above) over a 6-year period.

high_cholesterol_population.description: Total number of men with high serum cholesterol (245 mg per 100 mL and above) over a 6-year period.

low_cholesterol_cases.description: Number of coronary disease cases among men with lower serum cholesterol (less than 210 mg per 100 mL) over a 6-year period.

low_cholesterol_population.description: Total number of men with lower serum cholesterol (less than 210 mg per 100 mL) over a 6-year period.
